question: Company A sells a board game. According to the chart, which of the following statements is true?

options.A: The latest year shows a sharp decline compared to previous years.
options.B: Copies sold per year have been strictly increasing over the entire period.
options.C: The chart only shows sales up to part of the latest year, so it does not justify conclusions about the full-year trend.
options.D: The latest year has the lowest copies sold in the entire period.

explanation: The latest year in the chart only includes about 2 months of data, while previous years show full 12‑month totals. Comparing the partial-year value (372 copies) directly to full-year values like 1,999 copies creates the illusion of a sharp decline. We cannot draw full‑year conclusions from this incomplete aggregate.
